reviews.title: Recommend for all
reviews.body: Makes hunting and camping civilized. This portable shower and a 5 gallon bucket is great. Fill the bucket 1/3 way up. Add 2 gallons of water brought to a boil. I did in batches on camp stove, than top of bucket till your desired temp is reached. All set to go. Hose is long enough, l’ m 6’2. Sure the pressure is not strong, but adequate. Can’t believe how refreshed I felt afterwards. Looking forward to use again in the spring. No more bracing and shock, from a cold stream.

reviews.title: I'm going to be able to use this in many different applications while camping
reviews.body: This actually helps me solve my RV needs of having to install a sink and faucet

reviews.title: Works Great!
reviews.body: Absolutely love this shower so far! My husband and I have used this device for 1 week so far each taking a shower every  day. With this Corona virus thing we decided to take our RV to friends property to camp we have 30 amp power source but not direct water hook-up.  We can fill up a bucket of water to use inside our RV shower stall. Then use heated water for adding hot. We followed the easy instructions for hook-up. The pump was already charged when we got it and have only charged it once in a week. We used our vehicle charging port and a blue light comes on so you know when its charged. I wasnt sure how long the charge would last and certainly dont want to run out in middle of shower but even after 1 week with 2 showers a day only had to charge 1 time. A 5 gallon bucket works great filling 2/3 with well water then adding 2 tea kettles of Hot. We did figure out to fill the next person's bucket right away so it can get more room temperature but 1 time the 2 kettles of Hot was actually really a hot shower. Liked that the battery on off switch is separate out of the water and hooks onto the hose but it falls off sometimes so this is why the 4 stars. We just lean the hose over our existing shower nozzles and use 1 hand to hold device and rinse and turn it off and on during shampoo and washing. It has great water pressure too!  So far happy camping and nice warm shower feels so good . Will update as we are primitive camping for most of summer!

reviews.title: Great remote camp shower
reviews.body: Colorado remote camping trip was great and showers helped alot. Pump pressure was great battery was great but switch stuck in the on position. This pump and a 5 gallon bucket of hot water and you are in business.

reviews.title: Works well!
reviews.body: We bought this for camping and it worked really well. The water pressure is decent and it's easy to use. You can push the gray button to turn it on and then again to turn it off so you don't need to hold down the button. The only drawback is that you can't get the button wet (which is hard to do when you're standing in a small shower space)-- we ended up wrapping a ziploc bag around it with a twist tie and it worked well.

reviews.title: It'll get you and your gear clean!
reviews.body: We've used it on four camping / kayaking trips so far. It works great. It doesn't have the pressure of our home shower but it's more than enough to get you clean. We also use it to wash off our kayaking gear and boats at the end of the day. Love not having the sand and grit from the river on and in the car.  We have dropped the pump in a pail of river water, but we've also dropped in the river with no problem. The battery life has been really good. Only con is the wire for the on off button is really too short. You can't really hold the controller while using the shower and you can't set on something unless it's the ground or something really low to the ground. You got a watch it because it will find it's way into the water.

reviews.title: I returned it but great product
reviews.body: I had to return because it didn’t fit my camping needs but the water pressure was great and I really wanted to keep it. The two batteries charged, held a charge and ran for as long as I needed it for.

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised
reviews.body: I bought this for camping but it ended up being a life saver when my home hot water heater quit working. It pumps less than 1gpm but the water stream was way stronger than I expected, I have thick long hair and I had no problems rinsing the shampoo out. It is a bit awkward, it would be better if the cord from the battery to the pump were much longer so you could have the on/off switch higher up and easier to reach but it's definitely workable. I found it worked best for the pump if I filled a 5 gallon bucket with water even though I only used about half of the water. I didn't notice that charging it from a wall outlet was not an option and I'm not sure if they're all like that or not but that would be the only reason I might purchase a different one in the future. The battery lasted plenty long enough and when I recharged it only took about half the time to recharge so seems like there would be enough for at least 2 showers on one charge. Overall I'm pleased with it.

reviews.title: Great for camping and emergencies, okay for giving showers to bed-bound patients.
reviews.body: I primarily bought this so that my family could still shower in case of an earthquake, but I quickly realized that you can also use this to shower bed-bound geriatric patients. The only complaint I have is that there’s no wall adapter included in the box and that it’s not suitable for wall outlet use. Otherwise, it’s pretty good. Relatively easy to use and it’s actually got really good pressure. 7/10

reviews.title: GOOD PRODUCT
reviews.body: I like that is easy to assemble the battery takes a long time to charge the first time it works well I use it to rinse the showers and to give my dogs a bath the only bad thing that I can say about this product is that the extra battery is faulty and did not work other than that is a good portable shower.

reviews.title: Will use it multiple times
reviews.body: This portable shower head save my life during my confinement period! In Chinese culture, you can only shower with ginger boiled water instead of regular tap water during the first 30 days after you delivered a baby. So, I would put the boiled ginger water in a bucket and shower with it. I struggled to shower the first few times because the water was too hot and I had to wait for it to cool it down or I had to use a cup to pour the water onto my body. So this product saves my life! I could just put the pump into the bucket of ginger water and shower normally!! It basically made my day every time when I shower with it! One thing that I didn’t like was it could not suck the last few inches of the water in the bucket unless you tilt the bucket. So I just poured the last few inches of water directly onto my body.

reviews.title: Works well, sometimes the pump struggles to get water flowing at first.
reviews.body: for camping, got this shower, tested at home and discovered that it will suit my needs well with the caveat that it has an issue where the pump will turn on but flow will not start without having to turn it back off and on again a couple times. once working, it provides just enough of a shower stream to wet down, turn off, lather up, and then turn back on to rinse off. was able to to complete a head to toe wash with 5 gallons of water doing it Navy style(water only running while rinsing or wetting down) haven't had the charge run out while using yet, and the usb charging makes it easy to charge pretty much anywhere you can find a USB port.

reviews.title: Easy to use. Works well.
reviews.body: My husband broke his ankle and was stuck to tub baths for a few months, bought this and it worked great. Not the pressure you get from the regular shower but good enough pressure to wash hair. I also used this for my dogs and it worked very well.

reviews.title: The Best Camping Shower Except For...
reviews.body: Except for the missing pause button. Like what is found on most household shower heads. This is so good that it will go through 5 gallons of water in a few minutes.  As it is, you need to continuously turn off the pump at the battery to pause the water flow. The battery is not waterproof so that's risky, especially since the suction tube is very short compared to the shower hose. My solution is to leave the shower head submerged in the bucket  so that the water recycles. It has excellent pressure and you feel like you've taken a real shower. I would give it a 5-star rating if it had a pause function.

reviews.title: Amazingly Versatile Little Pump - So Handy!
reviews.body: -----------------------------------------------------Wish we had this in my old backpacking days!I bought this item when my hot water heater failed.  I brought an induction hot-plate into my bathroom and simply filled a large, clean container with warm water, dropped in the pump and showered!  And so versatile!... I had a bad leak in a kitchen faucet. To keep the kitchen functioning until I could make full repairs, the leak was being caught in a large container.  I was doing a LOT of bailing.  That got old fast!  This little pump came to my rescue!The pump can handle just about any small pumping (or bailing) job, or liquid transfer.It is incredibly small and light, and easy to charge and use.With a flow rate of just under 1 GPM and a pressure of maybe just under 1/2 PSI , you're not going to power wash your deck with it, but it worked great for the kind of chores I've described.  Run time is truly impressive.  I've put at least 50 gallons through it and the battery still shows no sign of weakening.  I'm still running on the first of two included rechargeable batteries.Like ANY pump, it can cavitate (push the air initially in it around in circles, instead of move the liquid), especially if the source reservoir is shallow, so you may have to turn it on its side to get the air out, and it helps to start with the outlet down near the liquid level until liquid flow starts (which you will feel and hear).  But possible cavitation is common to most any rotary pump, it is not a design flaw.I like it!

reviews.title: Better than expected
reviews.body: This product worked better than expected. It allowed for us to conserve water by shutting off and turning on, and only required approximately 3 to 5 gallons per person for a full shower while camping.

reviews.title: No adjustment from 3.5 liter per minute flow rate. Pump is not self-priming.
reviews.body: This is a great rechargeable portable shower. It's nice that it has a base that is only 55 mm. It's small enough to fit some water jugs. The base contains a coarse filter, inlet, and the pump. The battery connects using a DC plug from a battery that's clipped onto the hose away from the base.One thing this can't do is adjust the flow rate from 3.5 liters per minute. It's either on or off. It would have been nice if there was a way to adjust the flow rate to conserve water and battery. What if you only had 3.5 liters to shower with? That's a one minute shower!You could cut into the wires and install a voltage regulator. By regulating the voltage that the DC motor sees, you can adjust the flow rate. This isn't a difficult or expensive modification to do.Also, the pump is not self-priming. Therefore, the pump needs to be submerged in water.While it's a great shower kit, I returned it for the lack of flow adjustment and because the pump is not self-priming. (There are some on Amazon that have these features.)

reviews.title: Hall of Fame product
reviews.body: This is one of my favorite things I've ever bought on Amazon. During the process of renovating a house I needed a temp shower while rebuilding my bathroom. The reno took way longer than anticipated and I had to travel a bunch for work and so I ended up using this thing as my main shower *for two years*. The two batteries are clutch. I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.title: Great little pump
reviews.body: Took this camping and worked fantastic. I needed the hose longer, so I put another one on the end and taping it to make sure it didn't come apart. I put the pump in a mesh bag and tied a rope on it, and another on to the on off switch, because the stream has a big washed out edge making the length of the hose short. It worked better than I expected. I filled the 5 gallon buckets and took showers with it. We go wilderness camping, not another soak around.

reviews.title: Must buy
reviews.body: Amazing! A must for needed showers when camping, washing dog, absolutely  love it. I had bought  a prior where battery was submissive and went out shortly after. I love having the external batteries.

reviews.title: Manguera azul
reviews.body: Me encantó tiene muy buena presión y sirve para diferentes cosas como camping o para uso de peluquería como es mi caso.

reviews.title: Works great, just get it.
reviews.body: Works perfectly and set up is quick and easy. We've used it for showering while camping and also for washing dishes while camping. I've also used it for watering plants on my porch, works great.

reviews.title: Love this thing
reviews.body: This thing is amazing. I brought it to a camping music festival in the middle of summer and everyone was so jealous! I was able to take a cool shower every single day and did not fully kill one battery. I even stared it a few times with others. It was probably used for 30-45 minutes over the course of four days and did not die. Even if it did, I still has a backup battery, which is great. I brought a five gallon bucket to use as my water container. I'll update about its longevity once I've used it a few times.

reviews.title: Enough battery to last us couple of days in festival
reviews.body: Worked great for our camping trip. Went to Big Dub 2026 and going again this year. This worked for us to use as aportable shower in a shower tent for about 7 people in our group. This year will be using this again.

reviews.body: I live in my rv full time and use the shower for storage, so it’s always a pain to have to unload the shower to take a shower.  I bought this because when I used jugs of water it’s a MESS; I used /trial it for the first time yesterday.  It works as intended, I didn’t use too much water since this was a trial, but the battery =on/off worked fine, it seems to have reasonable water pressure, and the battery lasted, although I only used 1.5 gallons.The only thing that kept me from giving this FIVE ⭐️ was I which the battery was either waterproof or had a waterproof thingie to put it in && the power cord from the pump to the battery should have been at least as long as the hose from the pump to the sprayer.

reviews.title: Washing dogs
reviews.body: This product does exactly as described by the seller. Perfect for washing my dogs. Water pressure is ok.

reviews.title: Works But…
reviews.body: You can only charge the battery at1 two 2 amps or you kill the batteries. Can charge from a laptop or a 5V Power Supply 1.5A USB Power Brick 1500mA USB AC. You can use a car charger just make sure it’s no more then 2 amp max.Other then that it has nice water pressure.

reviews.title: Works well- no battery replacements available
reviews.body: I’ve used the shower for a couple of years on a regular basis. It works as it described but the batteries are the issue. After the first year the batteries died. I contacted the company and they sent me a whole new shower with 2 batteries for free. Very good service in my opinion.It has been about 14 months since then and the new batteries are now dead. Since I can’t find replacement batteries anywhere, I’m giving the product 4 stars.I also have a similar product from Ivation. It is 5 years old and still works perfectly. If the Ivation shower could be turned off next to the shower head, I would not have bough anything else. Between the two types the Innhom uses less water and provides sufficient pressure while the Ivation uses more water and provides great water pressure. Just something to think about.

reviews.title: Great pump, lots of pressure
reviews.body: This is a great pump! We use this pump in a homemade water heater in our camper van. The water pressure is very strong. Having the battery/power switch outside the water is super convenient so I can easily turn the pump off and on without having to pull the pump out of the water. The small sprayer is convenient because it has an on/off lever that allows you to moderate, or stop, water flow without turning off the pump. The shower head is a nice spray but I'd like it better if it also had an on/off lever. I can't speak to longevity because we've only had it a month or so but so far, it's far exceeded my needs and expectations.

I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.body: Giving 4 stars only because the battery does not last long. It won’t take a charge anymore after only a handful of uses. However, it comes with another usb cable that you can plug directly to a power source.

reviews.title: It'll get you and your gear clean!
reviews.body: We've used it on four camping / kayaking trips so far. It works great. It doesn't have the pressure of our home shower but it's more than enough to get you clean. We also use it to wash off our kayaking gear and boats at the end of the day. Love not having the sand and grit from the river on and in the car.  We have dropped the pump in a pail of river water, but we've also dropped in the river with no problem. The battery life has been really good. Only con is the wire for the on off button is really too short. You can't really hold the controller while using the shower and you can't set on something unless it's the ground or something really low to the ground. You got a watch it because it will find it's way into the water.

reviews.title: Great value and function once you figure it out.
reviews.body: THis little thing is pretty great.  It is low cost, and works great once you get it to prime.  I found that I needed to leave the hose off, submerge it, turn it on, and let it prime first.  Then I could slide the hose on and it would run perfectly as long as it was submerged.  It has a surprisingly high flow rate for such a small unit, which is nice if you have tons of water.  If not, you will need to turn it on/off so you don't waste water.  Here in Arizona, we have a haul our water with us, so I have learned to take a 1 gallon shower.  Overall a great unit.

reviews.body: for camping, got this shower, tested at home and discovered that it will suit my needs well with the caveat that it has an issue where the pump will turn on but flow will not start without having to turn it back off and on again a couple times. once working, it provides just enough of a shower stream to wet down, turn off, lather up, and then turn back on to rinse off. was able to to complete a head to toe wash with 5 gallons of water doing it Navy style(water only running while rinsing or wetting down) haven't had the charge run out while using yet, and the usb charging makes it easy to charge pretty much anywhere you can find a USB port.

reviews.title: Works great!

It has been used at least 200 times by several family members and still works as good as the first time we tried it. It also makes things much easier when washing the dogs.  If I  had a complaint it would probably be the battery attachment, it doesn't stay hooked to the buckets I've tried.

reviews.title: Check to make sure you have all the pieces
reviews.body: I think the product works great. It took some time for the water to pump through at first but once it got started it was fine. But I didn’t notice until after my battery died that I didn’t receive the 2nd battery pack or the charging cord. Now I typically wouldn’t worry about the charging cord since everything has a USB charger and we have plenty laying around the house but seeing as how this device needs a specific one, it’s a problem. So now I’m left without a lifeless product until I can get it replaced.

reviews.title: Amazingly Versatile Little Pump - So Handy!
reviews.body: -----------------------------------------------------Wish we had this in my old backpacking days!I bought this item when my hot water heater failed.  I brought an induction hot-plate into my bathroom and simply filled a large, clean container with warm water, dropped in the pump and showered!  And so versatile!... I had a bad leak in a kitchen faucet. To keep the kitchen functioning until I could make full repairs, the leak was being caught in a large container.  I was doing a LOT of bailing.  That got old fast!  This little pump came to my rescue!The pump can handle just about any small pumping (or bailing) job, or liquid transfer.It is incredibly small and light, and easy to charge and use.With a flow rate of just under 1 GPM and a pressure of maybe just under 1/2 PSI , you're not going to power wash your deck with it, but it worked great for the kind of chores I've described.  Run time is truly impressive.  I've put at least 50 gallons through it and the battery still shows no sign of weakening.  I'm still running on the first of two included rechargeable batteries.Like ANY pump, it can cavitate (push the air initially in it around in circles, instead of move the liquid), especially if the source reservoir is shallow, so you may have to turn it on its side to get the air out, and it helps to start with the outlet down near the liquid level until liquid flow starts (which you will feel and hear).  But possible cavitation is common to most any rotary pump, it is not a design flaw.I like it!

Straight out of the box the product had 2 batteries a pump and of course the shower head, my previous device had the battery and the pump integrated into one module.  Charging was simple however, what I didn't like if that you have the pump and the battery sort of together, the pump gets submerged in water while the battery has to stay out of the water.  The pressure generated by the pump was mediocre in comparison IVATION CAMP SHOWER HEAD that this replaced.  What broke the camel's back, so to speak, is that the hose that went into the shower head and the shower head separated from each other 2 days into use.  I put the hose back into the shower head thinking that it was a fluke, however, the same thing happened the next day.  This went back to Amazon for a full refund.  If you're looking to a good camping showerhead I highly recommend the one made by IVATION.

On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

reviews.title: Stops working within a couple of months
reviews.body: Has great power if the battery lasts! You don’t really get a year out of this product! If you use it often anyway. I had to have batteries replaced and sent to me on this and one works then doesn’t work two weeks later. The batteries are what makes this item not worth buying!!!

reviews.title: Water dosent reach shower head...no pressure.
reviews.body: Dosent have enough power for the water to even come out of the showers head the battery pack is worthless and doesn’t work. Great idea but the pump inside is not strong enough.

reviews.title: You are spending over $40 for about 3 to 5 low pressure showers
reviews.body: This company has continuously provided a faulty battery for over 5 years now.  The company changes their name, then resells the same faulty product, that has a different color. It's the same worthless product. When the battery dies after you've used it a handful of times, you have no options, your $40 is gone. You cannot buy replacement batteries that are reliable, the device cannot be powered by other means.This is a Scam and it's a real shame that it is allowed to continue for so many years.

reviews.title: It did Not last
reviews.body: I wish this item would have continued to work , it was great while lasted. It lasted for 2 weekend camping trips . The on/off button stop working and then it would not charge.  I was so aggravated that I just threw it away .

reviews.title: Works only when it wants to.
reviews.body: Battery some times works, other times it doesn't or it'll randomly turn on.

reviews.title: Doesn’t work.
reviews.body: Tried to charge the batteries. Nothing! The lights came on the one but just flashed continuously. The other battery wouldn’t take a charge. Sad because we needed this for our camping trip. Luckily...I guess.. we have the 5 gallon bag shower. Just a pain to use. We were trying to step it up a little. 🤨

reviews.title: Does not work
reviews.body: The battery did not work and would not take a charge to operate the device

reviews.title: Battery only works for about 10 seconds then is dead.
reviews.body: This never worked for longer than 10 seconds from the time I purchased it. And I never received two batteries. Very disappointed for the money I paid. ☹

reviews.title: FIRE HAZARD!! Battery blew while my fiance and I were sleeping! WE COULD HAVE DIED!!
reviews.body: What i didn't like??? IT ALMOST KILLED ME AND MY LADY!

reviews.title: charging cable stopped working
reviews.body: this product was as described but without the charging cable working the entire system is useless. I really needed this to work, it's very unfortunate. It worked to charge some of one pack then stopped, i tried charging it in a car as well as with 5v or below charging ports. Never charged it above the 5v as instructed. very disappointed.

reviews.title: Failed When Needed It
reviews.body: We bought this for use during power outages. We even bought a spare battery. Both batteries though plugged in prior to the storm and allegedly charged failed to operate when we went to use them. Forget this electric garbage and get yourself a good old fashioned hanging bag style.  (And no you can't use the name 'Electric Garbage' for your band name I already called it!)

reviews.title: Doesn’t work
reviews.body: Doesn’t work. Battery wont charge. Waste of money

reviews.title: Worked 2 times 5 gallons each. Stopped halfway through the 3rd use
reviews.body: Pump went bad. Not the batteries

reviews.title: Batteries would not charge
reviews.body: Used it once, ran great, but the batteries would not charge.  They have a standard USB on one end of the charging cable, with a specialized plug on the battery end.  If you wiggled the cable in the battery, it might start it's flashing light indicating it was charging, but it would quit and go dark as soon as you turned loose of it.  Neither battery charged, so it was one and done.  Batteries are terrible, but the pump was adequate.

reviews.title: Battery dead only 1 year.
reviews.body: Battery dead only 1 year.

reviews.title: Battery does not hold charge past 1 day
reviews.body: Bought this to go camping. Charged it and tried it an hour after charge and it worked. However, after I charged it and then waited to use it a day later there was no power. Absolutely, do not buy this product as it is a piece of crap. Also, warranty is awful and they will not let me return it. Lost my money.

reviews.title: Product is cheap
reviews.body: We bought these, and by the time we went to use them. It was to late to return them. They don’t work! We charged the batteries over night. Went to use them on a camping trip. Wouldn’t work!

reviews.title: Doesn't last.
reviews.body: Worked great until the batteries failed. I had it for 4 1/2 weeks and all of a sudden they turned themselves on then died.

reviews.title: Battery cord is too short
reviews.body: Cord for the battery/control pack is way too short, this will facilitate water into dripping and getting into the battery pack.

reviews.title: Absolutely worthless
reviews.body: Loud, no spray power at all . Looks like a cheap children's toy. The two batteries were a good concept but only one worked so defeated the purpose of two. Don't recommend wasting money on this

reviews.title: Quit working after about 6 times used!
reviews.body: I was excited to get this. First time worked well, but tube does not stay connected. Tape will not help it. Have to keep untwisting the hose as well. Keeps kinking up. But after 6 times it just completely quit working. Cannot get it to stop kinking so water does not go through to shower head. Great idea but poorly made and waste of money! Too expensive to quit working so soon! Batteries worked okay but discharge quickly. I kept having to use second battery to finish shower. Constantly had to reconnect tube because tape would not even keep it connected to pump.

reviews.title: One of the two batteries does not work
reviews.body: It works ok and I would be happy but one of the batteries is DOA , does not charge or turn on and can't find a way to contact. So not happy would not buy again.

reviews.title: No Shut off Button
reviews.body: Well NOT HAPPY! I bought this as a replacement for another brand cause I liked the color. I used it for the 1st time today n I was in the “tub” n soaped up before I realized I couldn’t shut it off without disconnecting the battery!!! I camp off grid n need to conserve water! Would NOT buy again.

reviews.title: Absolute trash
reviews.body: I bought this to replace another identical ( and I mean identical aside from name) camp shower we bought months ago that worked great for 4 months then died (pump went out).  Irritating but long enough it could be my problem.  So, I was going to repurchase (to assure identical batteries) but that product and business vanished.  Now,  the same product is here under new branding, so I bought it.  Well, the pump went out after ONE USE.  No debris, no hair, no excessive temps, it just died.  So yeah, just FYI......

reviews.title: Oh well
reviews.body: The batteries charged up just fine but unfortunately I am having no luck getting it to pump water. It sounds like it is trying really hard but sadly it’s not pumping water. I guess I got a dud. Will return it for a replacement.

reviews.title: Do not purchase
reviews.body: Never worked. No charging cable. Would never recommend to anyone.

reviews.title: No shut off at the nozzle
reviews.body: It wouldn't recommend this if you have a limited water supply.  There is no easy shut off at the shower handle like others have.  Once you turn the pump on, the water is spraying full force.  It's not easy to keep reaching for the battery to turn it off.  I wasted most of my water.

reviews.title: Batteries
reviews.body: Batteries  don’t charge anymore after just 5 uses

reviews.title: The product is not water proof !!!
reviews.body: So the item is not water proof ! Yet it's for water/showers ? It's chargeable but cannot touch water the chord is also so small it almost doesn't even make it out of a five gallon bucket .. if the chords for the product was much longer it would make the not water proof (charging part) make a little more sence.  Not satisfied at all

reviews.title: The product stopped working after a month.
reviews.body: The product stopped working after a month. The battery doesn’t turn on and the pump stopped working

reviews.title: Batteries won't turn off
reviews.body: This worked well the first time i used it. But after a couple of uses the power button on the battery packs don't work so it doesn't turn off unless you unplug it.

reviews.title: Poor quality.  Complicated set up.
reviews.body: I purchased this product to replace another camp shower head thinking that it would be better based on product reviews and the design of the pump.  Boy was I wrong!  Straight out of the box the product had 2 batteries a pump and of course the shower head, my previous device had the battery and the pump integrated into one module.  Charging was simple however, what I didn't like if that you have the pump and the battery sort of together, the pump gets submerged in water while the battery has to stay out of the water.  The pressure generated by the pump was mediocre in comparison IVATION CAMP SHOWER HEAD that this replaced.  What broke the camel's back, so to speak, is that the hose that went into the shower head and the shower head separated from each other 2 days into use.  I put the hose back into the shower head thinking that it was a fluke, however, the same thing happened the next day.  This went back to Amazon for a full refund.  If you're looking to a good camping showerhead I highly recommend the one made by IVATION.

reviews.title: It works well, when it chooses to work.
reviews.body: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

reviews.title: Only 1 battery works
reviews.body: Works good but 1 battery was DOA

reviews.title: Just quit mid shower
reviews.body: This worked great for one shower and then quit 10 seconds into the second use. Tried recharging both batteries, neither worked. Left me showerless all weekend.

reviews.title: Good pump but Battery defective
reviews.body: The pump works good and produces a nice flow but after multiple use one of the two battery died. And there are no replacement batteries offered by the company.

reviews.title: Cord is to short, buy one with a longer cord and hose.
reviews.body: I bought this and forgot I had it. We went camping last weekend and took it with us. We loved that it came with two batteries. We loved that it worked, and worked well. The two thing we hated was that that cord that’s attached to the motor was not long enough for a 8 gallon bucket. And the hose was not long enough to reach the top of the tent. Buy one with a longer cord and hose.

reviews.title: Batteries Never Worked total bummer
reviews.body: I bought this shower months ago to use at our cabin in the woods during our traditional Thanksgiving dinner. Tried to use it for dishes and neither battery worked and also would not charge. I used a proper charger as stated. I would love to be able to return it but didnt even try to use it till recent. Any suggestions or if this seller would replace the batteries. Not sure what to do. Be careful and test is as soon as you get it as to not end up like me.

reviews.title: Product does not work as described
reviews.body: This is the second time I purchase this shower product the first time it lasted 2 years this time it last only three showers the batteries stopped working I can't even get a hold of the manufacturer I wouldn't recommend this product to no one

reviews.title: Battery Stopped Working After 3 Months
reviews.body: One of the batteries stopped working after 3 months. Replacement batteries aren't available. This was a waste of money.

reviews.title: This product would be great if the on/off switch worked
reviews.body: I really liked this shower the first 2 uses, but on the third use the on off switch quit working. I had to unplug the battery unit to turn the shower off. This was tricky since I needed to shut it off before the water level in my bucket got too low and would burn up the motor. I would be happy if I could get a replacement battery pack/switch.

reviews.title: Worked the first couple of times
reviews.body: This worked great at first but on the third use the power button stopped working and just stays on now so have to plug in and unplug the battery when using. Needs better quality control. I’ll probably see if they’ll fix or replace the battery.

reviews.title: Battery not waterproof.
reviews.body: Battery not waterproof if it gets wet in the shower the on off switch won't shut off.

reviews.title: Bad and very cheaply made on/off switch
reviews.body: Both of the batteries with the on/off button broke-down after about 5 or 6 use and now we have to keep disconnecting the power cord from the pump in the middle of the use to stop the water !!!! Otherwise the pump doesn’t stop !!!!!!

reviews.title: It could be better...
reviews.body: So far it works, with a few issues. 1) The cord on the battery is too short. Makes it very inconvenient to turn off and on while showering. 2) The on/off button is kind of a pain. You have to hold it down for about 4 seconds to turn it on each time you turn it off. In the middle of a shower it's annoying. 3) The biggest problem we have is that there is no way to control the spray volume or pressure. It puts out plenty of water but it goes through 5 gallons really fast. It's a challenge to get 2 decent showers from 5 gallons of water. If you have an unlimited supply of water then I guess it would be fine. 4) The clip that holds the shower head is poorly designed. If you unclip it during your shower, reclipping it is a hassle... especially if you have soap in your eyes!All-in-all it's OK, but it could be a lot better. I'm sorry we paid $40 for this product.

reviews.title: Great, but needs a few modifications to be perfect
reviews.body: Since you're not supposed to get the battery wet, I found myself putting it in a ziplock when using. The power cord needs to be longer to get it out of the way. The way to turn on this thing is to hold the button on the battery which makes zero sense considering you're not supposed to get the battery wet. They need to provide a waterproof power switch somewhere else. Also, make the shower tube longer. I have to bend down to keep the pump at the bottom of the bucket.

reviews.title: New battery pack style difficult to install and remove
reviews.body: 11/2022 Update:  Unfortunately the company has changed the design of the battery packs and the new style is difficult to remove and install on the camp shower.  I will not be purchasing more.  3 battery packs have failed of the 4 that I own.  One more star removed from this review.Original review: I use this in my house call pet grooming business, to help mix and distribute shampoo and conditioner onto the pets, as well as rinse them.  I replaced the opaque hose with a clear hose (from local hardware store), and I don't use the sprayer nozzle (as it frightens the pets), I just wrap a rubberized hair band around the end of the hose to make it easier to grasp.  I like having the clear hose, as it makes it easier to make sure the hose is getting dry and not growing mold. I use it at least once or twice a week and it has lasted two years before one of the battery packs failed (it would be nice to be able to buy battery packs separately). The pump is quiet, and efficient at delivering the product/rinse water onto the pets.  I have bought 2 units to have spare batteries around, although one battery pack can usually last through one large dog.  I like to have back up chargers in my car and house, and back up batteries on hand in my grooming kit.  I don't leave the batteries out in the car heat and freezing weather, but bring them into the house to protect the battery life. One star off because I would really love it if they could make the black cord that attaches to the battery pack longer by about 12" (and a clear hose and housing and sprayer head would be awesome!) I actually link this product on my business Facebook page to recommend to my clients and friends for bathing their own dogs.  I have gotten feedback that it is also a great toy for the the grandkids, for washing cars, and camping!

reviews.title: it does work
reviews.body: followed the instruction, charged the batteries until stop flashing,  it would turn on, however no water pumped out.updatedcharged the battery overnight on a wall usb outlet anyway. turn it on, the water pump out from the hose, still no water from the shower head. clean the shower head with small needle. It finally works.check the battery status, it indicates 50% power level, even with overnight charge.

reviews.title: Worked great but battery wont charge
reviews.body: It worked great but the batteries will not charge and died rather quickly

reviews.title: The electric chord stopped working right away.
reviews.body: This shower has a very nice stream for a satisfying shower, and is fairly easy to use.  However The USB chord that runs the shower from a 12-volt stopped working right away, and I would like another.Also, I can't find the battery recharger and would like to purchase another recharger.

reviews.title: u need elc cord to use about 5ft 6ft long
reviews.body: need longer battery cord

reviews.title: This is super easy with knowing small things
reviews.body: This pump was perfect for camping and the battery had a good charge, didn't have to charge in a week of 1 shower per day, of about 7min use.But since purchasing in June one of the batteries has already taken a dump. Will not charge anymore.Suction is good but need to prime first by putting shower head lower then the extended height.

reviews.title: Gets the job done... barely
reviews.body: For a camp shower, this gets the job done. Barely. It works ok if you are not washing your hair. It will do the job if you have short hair. If you have long hair, don't even bother.Water output is low. You end up waiting to get wet, which actually causes you to use more water than normal, especially washing and rinsing hair.Pressure is low, and the water goes maybe 1 foot before it falls. That's why the shower head is angled down, because it wont' "shoot out" water at all. It works best when it can be pointed down, or close to down.The volume of water delivered is also low. The shower head is designed with only a few holes, so it seems like you are getting good flow... until you actually use it.The whole motor assembly sits in the water. I don't care how much you waterproof things, the motor is going to eventually get wet. It's nearly impossible to clean, and you have to pry off the lid to clean it. Bad design.The included mounting accessories are not super useful, for the most part you have to get creative with mounting.Battery life is excellent. The pump is lower power, and you can expect at least 5 showers on a charge.I've used this in a camp setting, in a pop-up shower enclosure. I have also used it in a home shower during a plumbing issue. Yes, it will get the job done, and it is better than nothing.  Ironically this camp shower uses twice as much water as it should because it takes far longer to shower than it should.If you plan on multiple showers during a longer camp outing, or need something for more than a single use, or want to use less water, this is not the best choice. For a zombie bug out bag... maybe.

reviews.title: Great shower, if it lasted
reviews.body: When i first bought the portable shower, I loved it. After sorting for the winter, the Batteries are dead, the shower does not work even though it looks brand new! I took good care of it too! It's lacking in quality and dependability

reviews.title: Has problems
reviews.body: It’s a great  idea for the beach. But pump stops pumping and the battery line could be longer.

reviews.title: I loved these. Until I didn't
reviews.body: I bought my first shower in 2022 and loved it so much I bought a second one in Oct of last year.  All of the batteries have now died. The shower was great, but on the batteries themselves, it says they are not waterproof and I guess mine got wet in the shower.

reviews.title: Overall battery life isn’t great.
reviews.body: Giving 4 stars only because the battery does not last long. It won’t take a charge anymore after only a handful of uses. However, it comes with another usb cable that you can plug directly to a power source.

reviews.title: Battery died
reviews.body: Bought this after moving to a new house. Due to the whole lock down deal we don't have gas service yet which means no hot water. So bought this to use with buckets of boiled water for hot showers. Been using it every day for over a month now and a week ago the battery that came with it died. Just won't charge at all and so taking off 1 star. It comes with a USB cable so you can power off power banks or the included 12V car plug. It doesn't work with a lot of power banks that I've tried. Eventually I was able to find 1 that worked so been using that. It probably tries to pull too much current on startup and the power bank's protection circuit cuts output. Definitely not as convenient as the included battery with button but does the job.Some people would rate this lower than 4 stars but honestly it works really well otherwise.

reviews.title: Update -battery won't charge - replaced by innhom
reviews.body: After two uses, the battery won't charge.  I bought this in Feb. I contacted the company, and there was no response.  So, I contacted the company  again and they wrote back after a few days.  They replaced my battery and it works now.

reviews.title: Works Great!
reviews.body: Absolutely love this shower so far! My husband and I have used this device for 1 week so far each taking a shower every  day. With this Corona virus thing we decided to take our RV to friends property to camp we have 30 amp power source but not direct water hook-up.  We can fill up a bucket of water to use inside our RV shower stall. Then use heated water for adding hot. We followed the easy instructions for hook-up. The pump was already charged when we got it and have only charged it once in a week. We used our vehicle charging port and a blue light comes on so you know when its charged. I wasnt sure how long the charge would last and certainly dont want to run out in middle of shower but even after 1 week with 2 showers a day only had to charge 1 time. A 5 gallon bucket works great filling 2/3 with well water then adding 2 tea kettles of Hot. We did figure out to fill the next person's bucket right away so it can get more room temperature but 1 time the 2 kettles of Hot was actually really a hot shower. Liked that the battery on off switch is separate out of the water and hooks onto the hose but it falls off sometimes so this is why the 4 stars. We just lean the hose over our existing shower nozzles and use 1 hand to hold device and rinse and turn it off and on during shampoo and washing. It has great water pressure too!  So far happy camping and nice warm shower feels so good . Will update as we are primitive camping for most of summer!

reviews.title: Simple shower system that works
reviews.body: I needed an outdoor shower system and this one was attractive. The price was a no brainer, so I ordered it.It worked very well for a month. And suddenly it stopped working. I don’t know if the pump went bad or if the battery on/off switch failed.I purchased a new pomp and it works again perfectly.

reviews.title: Great remote camp shower
reviews.body: Colorado remote camping trip was great and showers helped alot. Pump pressure was great battery was great but switch stuck in the on position. This pump and a 5 gallon bucket of hot water and you are in business.

reviews.title: It'll get you and your gear clean!
reviews.body: We've used it on four camping / kayaking trips so far. It works great. It doesn't have the pressure of our home shower but it's more than enough to get you clean. We also use it to wash off our kayaking gear and boats at the end of the day. Love not having the sand and grit from the river on and in the car.  We have dropped the pump in a pail of river water, but we've also dropped in the river with no problem. The battery life has been really good. Only con is the wire for the on off button is really too short. You can't really hold the controller while using the shower and you can't set on something unless it's the ground or something really low to the ground. You got a watch it because it will find it's way into the water.

reviews.title: The product delivered what I needed.
reviews.body: The directions for assembly were not complete, but I was able to figure it out.  The power of the pump was good.  Just read all directions and charge the battery before use.

reviews.title: I returned it but great product
reviews.body: I had to return because it didn’t fit my camping needs but the water pressure was great and I really wanted to keep it. The two batteries charged, held a charge and ran for as long as I needed it for.

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised
reviews.body: I bought this for camping but it ended up being a life saver when my home hot water heater quit working. It pumps less than 1gpm but the water stream was way stronger than I expected, I have thick long hair and I had no problems rinsing the shampoo out. It is a bit awkward, it would be better if the cord from the battery to the pump were much longer so you could have the on/off switch higher up and easier to reach but it's definitely workable. I found it worked best for the pump if I filled a 5 gallon bucket with water even though I only used about half of the water. I didn't notice that charging it from a wall outlet was not an option and I'm not sure if they're all like that or not but that would be the only reason I might purchase a different one in the future. The battery lasted plenty long enough and when I recharged it only took about half the time to recharge so seems like there would be enough for at least 2 showers on one charge. Overall I'm pleased with it.

reviews.title: Shower without power
reviews.body: Got this to use while our house water system had failed. Pump unit is good, more powerful than i thought, shower head very nice with a suction mount or a hook, good quality tubing between them (long enough for tall people). We had one of the two batteries fail out of the box, and the entire unit was replaced promptly (problems happen, it's how you handle them). The battery lasts a Shockingly long time, enough to pump ~15 to ~20 gallons at least (a nice long shower with this is about 3 gallons), which is very nice.

reviews.title: GOOD PRODUCT
reviews.body: I like that is easy to assemble the battery takes a long time to charge the first time it works well I use it to rinse the showers and to give my dogs a bath the only bad thing that I can say about this product is that the extra battery is faulty and did not work other than that is a good portable shower.

reviews.title: Would have given FIVE ⭐️ But . . .
reviews.body: I live in my rv full time and use the shower for storage, so it’s always a pain to have to unload the shower to take a shower.  I bought this because when I used jugs of water it’s a MESS; I used /trial it for the first time yesterday.  It works as intended, I didn’t use too much water since this was a trial, but the battery =on/off worked fine, it seems to have reasonable water pressure, and the battery lasted, although I only used 1.5 gallons.The only thing that kept me from giving this FIVE ⭐️ was I which the battery was either waterproof or had a waterproof thingie to put it in && the power cord from the pump to the battery should have been at least as long as the hose from the pump to the sprayer.

reviews.title: Works well, sometimes the pump struggles to get water flowing at first.
reviews.body: for camping, got this shower, tested at home and discovered that it will suit my needs well with the caveat that it has an issue where the pump will turn on but flow will not start without having to turn it back off and on again a couple times. once working, it provides just enough of a shower stream to wet down, turn off, lather up, and then turn back on to rinse off. was able to to complete a head to toe wash with 5 gallons of water doing it Navy style(water only running while rinsing or wetting down) haven't had the charge run out while using yet, and the usb charging makes it easy to charge pretty much anywhere you can find a USB port.

reviews.title: Works But…
reviews.body: You can only charge the battery at1 two 2 amps or you kill the batteries. Can charge from a laptop or a 5V Power Supply 1.5A USB Power Brick 1500mA USB AC. You can use a car charger just make sure it’s no more then 2 amp max.Other then that it has nice water pressure.

reviews.title: The Best Camping Shower Except For...
reviews.body: Except for the missing pause button. Like what is found on most household shower heads. This is so good that it will go through 5 gallons of water in a few minutes.  As it is, you need to continuously turn off the pump at the battery to pause the water flow. The battery is not waterproof so that's risky, especially since the suction tube is very short compared to the shower hose. My solution is to leave the shower head submerged in the bucket  so that the water recycles. It has excellent pressure and you feel like you've taken a real shower. I would give it a 5-star rating if it had a pause function.

reviews.title: Works well- no battery replacements available
reviews.body: I’ve used the shower for a couple of years on a regular basis. It works as it described but the batteries are the issue. After the first year the batteries died. I contacted the company and they sent me a whole new shower with 2 batteries for free. Very good service in my opinion.It has been about 14 months since then and the new batteries are now dead. Since I can’t find replacement batteries anywhere, I’m giving the product 4 stars.I also have a similar product from Ivation. It is 5 years old and still works perfectly. If the Ivation shower could be turned off next to the shower head, I would not have bough anything else. Between the two types the Innhom uses less water and provides sufficient pressure while the Ivation uses more water and provides great water pressure. Just something to think about.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.body: I purchased this product a year ago as a last resort when we had plumbing issues. It has been used at least 200 times by several family members and still works as good as the first time we tried it. It also makes things much easier when washing the dogs.  If I  had a complaint it would probably be the battery attachment, it doesn't stay hooked to the buckets I've tried.

reviews.title: Check to make sure you have all the pieces
reviews.body: I think the product works great. It took some time for the water to pump through at first but once it got started it was fine. But I didn’t notice until after my battery died that I didn’t receive the 2nd battery pack or the charging cord. Now I typically wouldn’t worry about the charging cord since everything has a USB charger and we have plenty laying around the house but seeing as how this device needs a specific one, it’s a problem. So now I’m left without a lifeless product until I can get it replaced.

reviews.title: Amazingly Versatile Little Pump - So Handy!
reviews.body: -----------------------------------------------------Wish we had this in my old backpacking days!I bought this item when my hot water heater failed.  I brought an induction hot-plate into my bathroom and simply filled a large, clean container with warm water, dropped in the pump and showered!  And so versatile!... I had a bad leak in a kitchen faucet. To keep the kitchen functioning until I could make full repairs, the leak was being caught in a large container.  I was doing a LOT of bailing.  That got old fast!  This little pump came to my rescue!The pump can handle just about any small pumping (or bailing) job, or liquid transfer.It is incredibly small and light, and easy to charge and use.With a flow rate of just under 1 GPM and a pressure of maybe just under 1/2 PSI , you're not going to power wash your deck with it, but it worked great for the kind of chores I've described.  Run time is truly impressive.  I've put at least 50 gallons through it and the battery still shows no sign of weakening.  I'm still running on the first of two included rechargeable batteries.Like ANY pump, it can cavitate (push the air initially in it around in circles, instead of move the liquid), especially if the source reservoir is shallow, so you may have to turn it on its side to get the air out, and it helps to start with the outlet down near the liquid level until liquid flow starts (which you will feel and hear).  But possible cavitation is common to most any rotary pump, it is not a design flaw.I like it!

reviews.title: Easy ti use.
reviews.body: Didn't like that the battery packs are not submersible. .

reviews.title: No adjustment from 3.5 liter per minute flow rate. Pump is not self-priming.
reviews.body: This is a great rechargeable portable shower. It's nice that it has a base that is only 55 mm. It's small enough to fit some water jugs. The base contains a coarse filter, inlet, and the pump. The battery connects using a DC plug from a battery that's clipped onto the hose away from the base.One thing this can't do is adjust the flow rate from 3.5 liters per minute. It's either on or off. It would have been nice if there was a way to adjust the flow rate to conserve water and battery. What if you only had 3.5 liters to shower with? That's a one minute shower!You could cut into the wires and install a voltage regulator. By regulating the voltage that the DC motor sees, you can adjust the flow rate. This isn't a difficult or expensive modification to do.Also, the pump is not self-priming. Therefore, the pump needs to be submerged in water.While it's a great shower kit, I returned it for the lack of flow adjustment and because the pump is not self-priming. (There are some on Amazon that have these features.)

reviews.title: Great pump, lots of pressure
reviews.body: This is a great pump! We use this pump in a homemade water heater in our camper van. The water pressure is very strong. Having the battery/power switch outside the water is super convenient so I can easily turn the pump off and on without having to pull the pump out of the water. The small sprayer is convenient because it has an on/off lever that allows you to moderate, or stop, water flow without turning off the pump. The shower head is a nice spray but I'd like it better if it also had an on/off lever. I can't speak to longevity because we've only had it a month or so but so far, it's far exceeded my needs and expectations.

reviews.title: Hall of Fame product
reviews.body: This is one of my favorite things I've ever bought on Amazon. During the process of renovating a house I needed a temp shower while rebuilding my bathroom. The reno took way longer than anticipated and I had to travel a bunch for work and so I ended up using this thing as my main shower *for two years*. The two batteries are clutch. I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.title: It works well, when it chooses to work.
reviews.body: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.

reviews.title: It could be better...
reviews.body: So far it works, with a few issues. 1) The cord on the battery is too short. Makes it very inconvenient to turn off and on while showering. 2) The on/off button is kind of a pain. You have to hold it down for about 4 seconds to turn it on each time you turn it off. In the middle of a shower it's annoying. 3) The biggest problem we have is that there is no way to control the spray volume or pressure. It puts out plenty of water but it goes through 5 gallons really fast. It's a challenge to get 2 decent showers from 5 gallons of water. If you have an unlimited supply of water then I guess it would be fine. 4) The clip that holds the shower head is poorly designed. If you unclip it during your shower, reclipping it is a hassle... especially if you have soap in your eyes!All-in-all it's OK, but it could be a lot better. I'm sorry we paid $40 for this product.

reviews.body: I've only used this a few times, but the flow was to high.  Empties a bucket quickly.  Not high pressure, just high flow.  I put a kitchen sprayer flow restricted in it, fit perfect.  Now it lasts long enough to rince soap off.

reviews.title: It uses a lot of water fast
reviews.body: Just a heads up it goes through about 2 gallons in 3 minuets if used consistently if you are being water conscious your best bet is to wet yourself down real fast. Try to keep as much of that water that has no soap in it to go back into whatever bucket it was coming out of and then turn it off soap yourself down turn it back on rinse yourself off for a few secondsturn it off scrub what you have left on your soap wise so you bring it all to the surface and then then rinse off a second time possibly a third but do it in phases of about 15 to 30 seconds for the whole process. It sucks because you have to keep turning the on and off button on and off ha ha ha, which I feel like with the amount of water, moisture, and soap on hands could lead to future issues with the apparatus. If water is not an issue and you’re close to a water source than this thing is clutch

reviews.title: Great value and function once you figure it out.
reviews.body: THis little thing is pretty great.  It is low cost, and works great once you get it to prime.  I found that I needed to leave the hose off, submerge it, turn it on, and let it prime first.  Then I could slide the hose on and it would run perfectly as long as it was submerged.  It has a surprisingly high flow rate for such a small unit, which is nice if you have tons of water.  If not, you will need to turn it on/off so you don't waste water.  Here in Arizona, we have a haul our water with us, so I have learned to take a 1 gallon shower.  Overall a great unit.

reviews.title: Works Great!
reviews.body: Absolutely love this shower so far! My husband and I have used this device for 1 week so far each taking a shower every  day. With this Corona virus thing we decided to take our RV to friends property to camp we have 30 amp power source but not direct water hook-up.  We can fill up a bucket of water to use inside our RV shower stall. Then use heated water for adding hot. We followed the easy instructions for hook-up. The pump was already charged when we got it and have only charged it once in a week. We used our vehicle charging port and a blue light comes on so you know when its charged. I wasnt sure how long the charge would last and certainly dont want to run out in middle of shower but even after 1 week with 2 showers a day only had to charge 1 time. A 5 gallon bucket works great filling 2/3 with well water then adding 2 tea kettles of Hot. We did figure out to fill the next person's bucket right away so it can get more room temperature but 1 time the 2 kettles of Hot was actually really a hot shower. Liked that the battery on off switch is separate out of the water and hooks onto the hose but it falls off sometimes so this is why the 4 stars. We just lean the hose over our existing shower nozzles and use 1 hand to hold device and rinse and turn it off and on during shampoo and washing. It has great water pressure too!  So far happy camping and nice warm shower feels so good . Will update as we are primitive camping for most of summer!

The only drawback is that you can't get the button wet (which is hard to do when you're standing in a small shower space)-- we ended up wrapping a ziploc bag around it with a twist tie and it worked well.

reviews.body: This never worked for longer than 10 seconds from the time I purchased it. And I never received two batteries. Very disappointed for the money I paid. ☹

reviews.title: Battery cord is too short
reviews.body: Cord for the battery/control pack is way too short, this will facilitate water into dripping and getting into the battery pack.

reviews.title: DO NOT BUY!!!
reviews.body: DO NOT BUY THIS!!!The cord has a short in it after using one time.I purchased it in September but just opened it last week and its already not working properly. Amazon says my return window has closed because of the timeframe.

reviews.title: The product is not water proof !!!
reviews.body: So the item is not water proof ! Yet it's for water/showers ? It's chargeable but cannot touch water the chord is also so small it almost doesn't even make it out of a five gallon bucket .. if the chords for the product was much longer it would make the not water proof (charging part) make a little more sence.  Not satisfied at all

reviews.title: Too bad, just didn't last for the money I paid, not even a full season of camping 😕
reviews.body: The short performance was 5 stars right out the door with excellent shower power, easy to use HOWEVER 7 showers later and it no longer works! We actually used it excessively for the short amount of time we had it. In regular camping season it would have been outside the exchange period also so I'd have to suggest finding a product with a more realistic trial for this kind of money! Too bad, I worldview given it another shot but I guess they made their $50 bucks so it didn't matter if they get repeat customers. What Shame!

reviews.title: It didn't last long at all.
reviews.body: Quit working within a week and the cords are thin and unreliable. Package came quick but I will not be buying again.

reviews.title: Cord is to short, buy one with a longer cord and hose.
reviews.body: I bought this and forgot I had it. We went camping last weekend and took it with us. We loved that it came with two batteries. We loved that it worked, and worked well. The two thing we hated was that that cord that’s attached to the motor was not long enough for a 8 gallon bucket. And the hose was not long enough to reach the top of the tent. Buy one with a longer cord and hose.

reviews.title: Bad and very cheaply made on/off switch
reviews.body: Both of the batteries with the on/off button broke-down after about 5 or 6 use and now we have to keep disconnecting the power cord from the pump in the middle of the use to stop the water !!!! Otherwise the pump doesn’t stop !!!!!!

reviews.title: It could be better...
reviews.body: So far it works, with a few issues. 1) The cord on the battery is too short. Makes it very inconvenient to turn off and on while showering. 2) The on/off button is kind of a pain. You have to hold it down for about 4 seconds to turn it on each time you turn it off. In the middle of a shower it's annoying. 3) The biggest problem we have is that there is no way to control the spray volume or pressure. It puts out plenty of water but it goes through 5 gallons really fast. It's a challenge to get 2 decent showers from 5 gallons of water. If you have an unlimited supply of water then I guess it would be fine. 4) The clip that holds the shower head is poorly designed. If you unclip it during your shower, reclipping it is a hassle... especially if you have soap in your eyes!All-in-all it's OK, but it could be a lot better. I'm sorry we paid $40 for this product.

reviews.title: Great, but needs a few modifications to be perfect
reviews.body: Since you're not supposed to get the battery wet, I found myself putting it in a ziplock when using. The power cord needs to be longer to get it out of the way. The way to turn on this thing is to hold the button on the battery which makes zero sense considering you're not supposed to get the battery wet. They need to provide a waterproof power switch somewhere else. Also, make the shower tube longer. I have to bend down to keep the pump at the bottom of the bucket.

reviews.title: New battery pack style difficult to install and remove
reviews.body: 11/2022 Update:  Unfortunately the company has changed the design of the battery packs and the new style is difficult to remove and install on the camp shower.  I will not be purchasing more.  3 battery packs have failed of the 4 that I own.  One more star removed from this review.Original review: I use this in my house call pet grooming business, to help mix and distribute shampoo and conditioner onto the pets, as well as rinse them.  I replaced the opaque hose with a clear hose (from local hardware store), and I don't use the sprayer nozzle (as it frightens the pets), I just wrap a rubberized hair band around the end of the hose to make it easier to grasp.  I like having the clear hose, as it makes it easier to make sure the hose is getting dry and not growing mold. I use it at least once or twice a week and it has lasted two years before one of the battery packs failed (it would be nice to be able to buy battery packs separately). The pump is quiet, and efficient at delivering the product/rinse water onto the pets.  I have bought 2 units to have spare batteries around, although one battery pack can usually last through one large dog.  I like to have back up chargers in my car and house, and back up batteries on hand in my grooming kit.  I don't leave the batteries out in the car heat and freezing weather, but bring them into the house to protect the battery life. One star off because I would really love it if they could make the black cord that attaches to the battery pack longer by about 12" (and a clear hose and housing and sprayer head would be awesome!) I actually link this product on my business Facebook page to recommend to my clients and friends for bathing their own dogs.  I have gotten feedback that it is also a great toy for the the grandkids, for washing cars, and camping!

reviews.title: u need elc cord to use about 5ft 6ft long
reviews.body: need longer battery cord

reviews.title: Gets the job done... barely
reviews.body: For a camp shower, this gets the job done. Barely. It works ok if you are not washing your hair. It will do the job if you have short hair. If you have long hair, don't even bother.Water output is low. You end up waiting to get wet, which actually causes you to use more water than normal, especially washing and rinsing hair.Pressure is low, and the water goes maybe 1 foot before it falls. That's why the shower head is angled down, because it wont' "shoot out" water at all. It works best when it can be pointed down, or close to down.The volume of water delivered is also low. The shower head is designed with only a few holes, so it seems like you are getting good flow... until you actually use it.The whole motor assembly sits in the water. I don't care how much you waterproof things, the motor is going to eventually get wet. It's nearly impossible to clean, and you have to pry off the lid to clean it. Bad design.The included mounting accessories are not super useful, for the most part you have to get creative with mounting.Battery life is excellent. The pump is lower power, and you can expect at least 5 showers on a charge.I've used this in a camp setting, in a pop-up shower enclosure. I have also used it in a home shower during a plumbing issue. Yes, it will get the job done, and it is better than nothing.  Ironically this camp shower uses twice as much water as it should because it takes far longer to shower than it should.If you plan on multiple showers during a longer camp outing, or need something for more than a single use, or want to use less water, this is not the best choice. For a zombie bug out bag... maybe.

reviews.title: Size matters
reviews.body: Wish the tubes were longerAnd the spray was strongerDid do the job just wishful thinking

reviews.title: Has problems
reviews.body: It’s a great  idea for the beach. But pump stops pumping and the battery line could be longer.

reviews.title: Tick Tock Made me try it
reviews.body: What can I say, the concept is simple, use it on the boat to rinse off. Could use a little more power but for what it is and does… nothing super fancy, would like a longer hose which I can buy from hardware store.

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised
reviews.body: I bought this for camping but it ended up being a life saver when my home hot water heater quit working. It pumps less than 1gpm but the water stream was way stronger than I expected, I have thick long hair and I had no problems rinsing the shampoo out. It is a bit awkward, it would be better if the cord from the battery to the pump were much longer so you could have the on/off switch higher up and easier to reach but it's definitely workable. I found it worked best for the pump if I filled a 5 gallon bucket with water even though I only used about half of the water. I didn't notice that charging it from a wall outlet was not an option and I'm not sure if they're all like that or not but that would be the only reason I might purchase a different one in the future. The battery lasted plenty long enough and when I recharged it only took about half the time to recharge so seems like there would be enough for at least 2 showers on one charge. Overall I'm pleased with it.

reviews.title: Would have given FIVE ⭐️ But . . .
reviews.body: I live in my rv full time and use the shower for storage, so it’s always a pain to have to unload the shower to take a shower.  I bought this because when I used jugs of water it’s a MESS; I used /trial it for the first time yesterday.  It works as intended, I didn’t use too much water since this was a trial, but the battery =on/off worked fine, it seems to have reasonable water pressure, and the battery lasted, although I only used 1.5 gallons.The only thing that kept me from giving this FIVE ⭐️ was I which the battery was either waterproof or had a waterproof thingie to put it in && the power cord from the pump to the battery should have been at least as long as the hose from the pump to the sprayer.

reviews.title: Check to make sure you have all the pieces
reviews.body: I think the product works great. It took some time for the water to pump through at first but once it got started it was fine. But I didn’t notice until after my battery died that I didn’t receive the 2nd battery pack or the charging cord. Now I typically wouldn’t worry about the charging cord since everything has a USB charger and we have plenty laying around the house but seeing as how this device needs a specific one, it’s a problem. So now I’m left without a lifeless product until I can get it replaced.

reviews.title: Hall of Fame product
reviews.body: This is one of my favorite things I've ever bought on Amazon. During the process of renovating a house I needed a temp shower while rebuilding my bathroom. The reno took way longer than anticipated and I had to travel a bunch for work and so I ended up using this thing as my main shower *for two years*. The two batteries are clutch. I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.title: Great little pump
reviews.body: Took this camping and worked fantastic. I needed the hose longer, so I put another one on the end and taping it to make sure it didn't come apart. I put the pump in a mesh bag and tied a rope on it, and another on to the on off switch, because the stream has a big washed out edge making the length of the hose short. It worked better than I expected. I filled the 5 gallon buckets and took showers with it. We go wilderness camping, not another soak around.

reviews.title: Missing parts
reviews.body: Mine didn't come with a USB charger cord but I was able to find a charger cord in one of my junk boxes and I must say this is a very nice shower! Works perfect

reviews.body: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. I can hear the motor running, it just won’t suck any water into the hose. I’ve tried both batteries after charging overnight, and I have the same problem, so I don’t think that is the issue. On the plus side, when it actually DOES work, it gives solid water pressure. Goes through a gallon of water in about 2-3 minutes. Overall, I would return it, but I stuck it up on a shelf after the second or third use and forgot about it, and I think I’m past the return window.
