reviews.title: It’s ok
reviews.body: It pumps so much so fast. Literally empties a gallon of water in seconds. You have to turn it off and on during your shower.

reviews.title: Works ok
reviews.body: When it is working properly, it works well but it would stop pumping at different times and you would have to turn it off then back on again

reviews.title: It uses a lot of water fast
reviews.body: Just a heads up it goes through about 2 gallons in 3 minuets if used consistently if you are being water conscious your best bet is to wet yourself down real fast. Try to keep as much of that water that has no soap in it to go back into whatever bucket it was coming out of and then turn it off soap yourself down turn it back on rinse yourself off for a few secondsturn it off scrub what you have left on your soap wise so you bring it all to the surface and then then rinse off a second time possibly a third but do it in phases of about 15 to 30 seconds for the whole process. It sucks because you have to keep turning the on and off button on and off ha ha ha, which I feel like with the amount of water, moisture, and soap on hands could lead to future issues with the apparatus. If water is not an issue and you’re close to a water source than this thing is clutch

reviews.title: Not for me.
reviews.body: I wouldn't buy it again. The start button is difficult to press, and it's bulky and inconvenient to use when you're actually showering.

reviews.title: Works Great!
reviews.body: Absolutely love this shower so far! My husband and I have used this device for 1 week so far each taking a shower every  day. With this Corona virus thing we decided to take our RV to friends property to camp we have 30 amp power source but not direct water hook-up.  We can fill up a bucket of water to use inside our RV shower stall. Then use heated water for adding hot. We followed the easy instructions for hook-up. The pump was already charged when we got it and have only charged it once in a week. We used our vehicle charging port and a blue light comes on so you know when its charged. I wasnt sure how long the charge would last and certainly dont want to run out in middle of shower but even after 1 week with 2 showers a day only had to charge 1 time. A 5 gallon bucket works great filling 2/3 with well water then adding 2 tea kettles of Hot. We did figure out to fill the next person's bucket right away so it can get more room temperature but 1 time the 2 kettles of Hot was actually really a hot shower. Liked that the battery on off switch is separate out of the water and hooks onto the hose but it falls off sometimes so this is why the 4 stars. We just lean the hose over our existing shower nozzles and use 1 hand to hold device and rinse and turn it off and on during shampoo and washing. It has great water pressure too!  So far happy camping and nice warm shower feels so good . Will update as we are primitive camping for most of summer!

reviews.title: Works well!
reviews.body: We bought this for camping and it worked really well. The water pressure is decent and it's easy to use. You can push the gray button to turn it on and then again to turn it off so you don't need to hold down the button. The only drawback is that you can't get the button wet (which is hard to do when you're standing in a small shower space)-- we ended up wrapping a ziploc bag around it with a twist tie and it worked well.

reviews.title: not as good as it appears in the video
reviews.body: the water took a long time to come out. then when it did it was drops I was so sad because i needed it for a camping trip.

Absolutely, do not buy this product as it is a piece of crap. Also, warranty is awful and they will not let me return it. Lost my money.

reviews.title: Product is cheap

reviews.title: Product arrived used
reviews.body: I began return process and I'm a week away from my camping trip I hope I find a good shower head since I received this one used. Found hair and calcium already built up in the creases.

reviews.title: Poor quality.  Complicated set up.
reviews.body: I purchased this product to replace another camp shower head thinking that it would be better based on product reviews and the design of the pump.  Boy was I wrong!  Straight out of the box the product had 2 batteries a pump and of course the shower head, my previous device had the battery and the pump integrated into one module.  Charging was simple however, what I didn't like if that you have the pump and the battery sort of together, the pump gets submerged in water while the battery has to stay out of the water.  The pressure generated by the pump was mediocre in comparison IVATION CAMP SHOWER HEAD that this replaced.  What broke the camel's back, so to speak, is that the hose that went into the shower head and the shower head separated from each other 2 days into use.  I put the hose back into the shower head thinking that it was a fluke, however, the same thing happened the next day.  This went back to Amazon for a full refund.  If you're looking to a good camping showerhead I highly recommend the one made by IVATION.

reviews.title: Camping
reviews.body: Es buena, súper útil, fácil de usar y muy cómoda pero las baterías son malas, no aguantan un baño, se descargan enseguida y no son duraderas, sino se usan dejan de duncionar

reviews.title: Works great when it works
reviews.body: We go camping nearly every weekend to mine for minerals. (So we get  pretty dirty!)  This camp shower works fantastic the first few times. After that it stopped working. Ordered a replacement and the same thing happened. Between 2 units I got about 10 weekends of showers out of them before deciding to try a different brand.

The two thing we hated was that that cord that’s attached to the motor was not long enough for a 8 gallon bucket. And the hose was not long enough to reach the top of the tent. Buy one with a longer cord and hose.

reviews.body: This pump was perfect for camping and the battery had a good charge, didn't have to charge in a week of 1 shower per day, of about 7min use.But since purchasing in June one of the batteries has already taken a dump. Will not charge anymore.Suction is good but need to prime first by putting shower head lower then the extended height.

reviews.title: Great shower, if it lasted
reviews.body: When i first bought the portable shower, I loved it. After sorting for the winter, the Batteries are dead, the shower does not work even though it looks brand new! I took good care of it too! It's lacking in quality and dependability

reviews.title: Recommend for all
reviews.body: Makes hunting and camping civilized. This portable shower and a 5 gallon bucket is great. Fill the bucket 1/3 way up. Add 2 gallons of water brought to a boil. I did in batches on camp stove, than top of bucket till your desired temp is reached. All set to go. Hose is long enough, l’ m 6’2. Sure the pressure is not strong, but adequate. Can’t believe how refreshed I felt afterwards. Looking forward to use again in the spring. No more bracing and shock, from a cold stream.

reviews.title: I'm going to be able to use this in many different applications while camping
reviews.body: This actually helps me solve my RV needs of having to install a sink and faucet

You can push the gray button to turn it on and then again to turn it off so you don't need to hold down the button. The only drawback is that you can't get the button wet (which is hard to do when you're standing in a small shower space)-- we ended up wrapping a ziploc bag around it with a twist tie and it worked well.

reviews.title: It'll get you and your gear clean!
reviews.body: We've used it on four camping / kayaking trips so far. It works great. It doesn't have the pressure of our home shower but it's more than enough to get you clean. We also use it to wash off our kayaking gear and boats at the end of the day. Love not having the sand and grit from the river on and in the car.  We have dropped the pump in a pail of river water, but we've also dropped in the river with no problem. The battery life has been really good. Only con is the wire for the on off button is really too short. You can't really hold the controller while using the shower and you can't set on something unless it's the ground or something really low to the ground. You got a watch it because it will find it's way into the water.

reviews.title: I returned it but great product
reviews.body: I had to return because it didn’t fit my camping needs but the water pressure was great and I really wanted to keep it. The two batteries charged, held a charge and ran for as long as I needed it for.

reviews.title: Pleasantly surprised
reviews.body: I bought this for camping but it ended up being a life saver when my home hot water heater quit working. It pumps less than 1gpm but the water stream was way stronger than I expected, I have thick long hair and I had no problems rinsing the shampoo out. It is a bit awkward, it would be better if the cord from the battery to the pump were much longer so you could have the on/off switch higher up and easier to reach but it's definitely workable. I found it worked best for the pump if I filled a 5 gallon bucket with water even though I only used about half of the water. I didn't notice that charging it from a wall outlet was not an option and I'm not sure if they're all like that or not but that would be the only reason I might purchase a different one in the future. The battery lasted plenty long enough and when I recharged it only took about half the time to recharge so seems like there would be enough for at least 2 showers on one charge. Overall I'm pleased with it.

reviews.title: Great for camping and emergencies, okay for giving showers to bed-bound patients.
reviews.body: I primarily bought this so that my family could still shower in case of an earthquake, but I quickly realized that you can also use this to shower bed-bound geriatric patients. The only complaint I have is that there’s no wall adapter included in the box and that it’s not suitable for wall outlet use. Otherwise, it’s pretty good. Relatively easy to use and it’s actually got really good pressure. 7/10

reviews.title: GOOD PRODUCT
reviews.body: I like that is easy to assemble the battery takes a long time to charge the first time it works well I use it to rinse the showers and to give my dogs a bath the only bad thing that I can say about this product is that the extra battery is faulty and did not work other than that is a good portable shower.

reviews.title: Will use it multiple times
reviews.body: This portable shower head save my life during my confinement period! In Chinese culture, you can only shower with ginger boiled water instead of regular tap water during the first 30 days after you delivered a baby. So, I would put the boiled ginger water in a bucket and shower with it. I struggled to shower the first few times because the water was too hot and I had to wait for it to cool it down or I had to use a cup to pour the water onto my body. So this product saves my life! I could just put the pump into the bucket of ginger water and shower normally!! It basically made my day every time when I shower with it! One thing that I didn’t like was it could not suck the last few inches of the water in the bucket unless you tilt the bucket. So I just poured the last few inches of water directly onto my body.

reviews.title: Works well, sometimes the pump struggles to get water flowing at first.
reviews.body: for camping, got this shower, tested at home and discovered that it will suit my needs well with the caveat that it has an issue where the pump will turn on but flow will not start without having to turn it back off and on again a couple times. once working, it provides just enough of a shower stream to wet down, turn off, lather up, and then turn back on to rinse off. was able to to complete a head to toe wash with 5 gallons of water doing it Navy style(water only running while rinsing or wetting down) haven't had the charge run out while using yet, and the usb charging makes it easy to charge pretty much anywhere you can find a USB port.

reviews.title: The Best Camping Shower Except For...
reviews.body: Except for the missing pause button. Like what is found on most household shower heads. This is so good that it will go through 5 gallons of water in a few minutes.  As it is, you need to continuously turn off the pump at the battery to pause the water flow. The battery is not waterproof so that's risky, especially since the suction tube is very short compared to the shower hose. My solution is to leave the shower head submerged in the bucket  so that the water recycles. It has excellent pressure and you feel like you've taken a real shower. I would give it a 5-star rating if it had a pause function.

reviews.title: Better than expected
reviews.body: This product worked better than expected. It allowed for us to conserve water by shutting off and turning on, and only required approximately 3 to 5 gallons per person for a full shower while camping.

reviews.title: No adjustment from 3.5 liter per minute flow rate. Pump is not self-priming.
reviews.body: This is a great rechargeable portable shower. It's nice that it has a base that is only 55 mm. It's small enough to fit some water jugs. The base contains a coarse filter, inlet, and the pump. The battery connects using a DC plug from a battery that's clipped onto the hose away from the base.One thing this can't do is adjust the flow rate from 3.5 liters per minute. It's either on or off. It would have been nice if there was a way to adjust the flow rate to conserve water and battery. What if you only had 3.5 liters to shower with? That's a one minute shower!You could cut into the wires and install a voltage regulator. By regulating the voltage that the DC motor sees, you can adjust the flow rate. This isn't a difficult or expensive modification to do.Also, the pump is not self-priming. Therefore, the pump needs to be submerged in water.While it's a great shower kit, I returned it for the lack of flow adjustment and because the pump is not self-priming. (There are some on Amazon that have these features.)

reviews.title: Hall of Fame product
reviews.body: This is one of my favorite things I've ever bought on Amazon. During the process of renovating a house I needed a temp shower while rebuilding my bathroom. The reno took way longer than anticipated and I had to travel a bunch for work and so I ended up using this thing as my main shower *for two years*. The two batteries are clutch. I always kept one on a charger and never had an issue. Water pressure is great. Even after finishing my bathroom, I've moved this thing in with my camping gear because it's just such a great product.

reviews.title: Great little pump
reviews.body: Took this camping and worked fantastic. I needed the hose longer, so I put another one on the end and taping it to make sure it didn't come apart. I put the pump in a mesh bag and tied a rope on it, and another on to the on off switch, because the stream has a big washed out edge making the length of the hose short. It worked better than I expected. I filled the 5 gallon buckets and took showers with it. We go wilderness camping, not another soak around.

reviews.title: Must buy
reviews.body: Amazing! A must for needed showers when camping, washing dog, absolutely  love it. I had bought  a prior where battery was submissive and went out shortly after. I love having the external batteries.

reviews.title: Manguera azul
reviews.body: Me encantó tiene muy buena presión y sirve para diferentes cosas como camping o para uso de peluquería como es mi caso.

reviews.title: Works great, just get it.
reviews.body: Works perfectly and set up is quick and easy. We've used it for showering while camping and also for washing dishes while camping. I've also used it for watering plants on my porch, works great.

reviews.title: Love this thing
reviews.body: This thing is amazing. I brought it to a camping music festival in the middle of summer and everyone was so jealous! I was able to take a cool shower every single day and did not fully kill one battery. I even stared it a few times with others. It was probably used for 30-45 minutes over the course of four days and did not die. Even if it did, I still has a backup battery, which is great. I brought a five gallon bucket to use as my water container. I'll update about its longevity once I've used it a few times.

reviews.title: Enough battery to last us couple of days in festival
reviews.body: Worked great for our camping trip. Went to Big Dub 2026 and going again this year. This worked for us to use as aportable shower in a shower tent for about 7 people in our group. This year will be using this again.

reviews.title: Works well- no battery replacements available
reviews.body: I’ve used the shower for a couple of years on a regular basis. It works as it described but the batteries are the issue. After the first year the batteries died. I contacted the company and they sent me a whole new shower with 2 batteries for free. Very good service in my opinion.It has been about 14 months since then and the new batteries are now dead. Since I can’t find replacement batteries anywhere, I’m giving the product 4 stars.I also have a similar product from Ivation. It is 5 years old and still works perfectly. If the Ivation shower could be turned off next to the shower head, I would not have bough anything else. Between the two types the Innhom uses less water and provides sufficient pressure while the Ivation uses more water and provides great water pressure. Just something to think about.
